title: General Audience Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a concise, non-technical summary of the proposed research project. Clearly articulate the project's goals, methods, and potential impact on melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, or treatment. Emphasize the relevance and significance of the project to a general audience. Avoid jargon and technical terms.

title: Technical Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a technical abstract summarizing the proposed research project. Include the background, specific aims, methods, and expected outcomes of the project. Highlight the significance of the research and its potential contribution to the field of melanoma research. Be concise and precise, using scientific terminology.

title: Project Description
generation_instructions: Develop a comprehensive project description detailing the background, specific aims, experimental design, methods, and statistical plan. Discuss the rationale for the project, including the gaps in current knowledge and how this research will address those gaps. Provide a clear, detailed overview of the experimental design, including methods, controls, and data analysis strategy. Describe the statistical methods for data analysis. Discuss potential challenges and address them.

title: Background and Specific Aims
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed background of the research area, including relevant literature and identify any knowledge gaps. Clearly state the specific aims and objectives of the proposed research and connect these with the research gaps identified in the background. Formulate a clear, testable hypothesis for this project.

title: Preliminary Data
generation_instructions: Present any preliminary data that supports the feasibility and rationale of the proposed research project. Explain the methods and results of these experiments and show how this data justifies the proposed aims. If applicable include pilot data supporting the proposed experimental design and methods.

title: Experimental Design and Methods
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed description of the experimental design and methods that will be used in the proposed research project. Describe the materials, procedures, and controls for each experiment. Explain the rationale for the choice of methods and their suitability to achieve the research aims, ensuring the methods are reproducible.

title: Statistical Plan
generation_instructions: Detail the statistical methods that will be used for data analysis. Include a description of the statistical tests, sample sizes, power calculations, and criteria for statistical significance. Describe how the data will be interpreted and how it will be used to draw conclusions relevant to the specific aims. Justify the choice of methods and their appropriateness.

title: Figures
generation_instructions: Provide a brief description of the figures and their relevance to the project description. Ensure the figures are clear and easy to understand, highlighting the project and research data effectively. Figures should be embedded within the text or provided at the end of the document.

title: Rationale/Fit with Key Criteria
generation_instructions: Explain how the proposed research project aligns with the key criteria of the MRA's call for proposals. Clearly state how the project addresses the priorities, including prevention, detection, diagnosis, and treatment of melanoma. Explain the research rationale, emphasizing the project's potential impact and its translational potential. Justify the selection of the research approach based on these criteria.

title: Potential for Clinical Impact
generation_instructions: Clearly explain the potential clinical impact of the proposed research. Describe how the research findings could be translated into tangible benefits for patients, such as new treatments, diagnostics, or prevention strategies for melanoma. Discuss the potential for future clinical trials or studies based on this project. Outline how the research could improve patient outcomes.
